violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

Deli meats observed not marked with the date of package opening. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared/package opening in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation/opening of package. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

ROP seafood salad meat observed thawing in the original package. Seafood salad meat will be used tomorrow. Packaging states to thaw under refrigeration if used immediately. Temperature of product observed at 34 degrees F and was removed from packaging during inspection for Oxygen to enter. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025
Refrigerator door gasket of the 1-door reach-in observed in poor repair, split away from the door. Gasket is to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
Refrigerator door gasket of the 1-door reach-in observed in poor repair, split away from the door. New gasket has been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024
The ceiling in areas of the kitchen is covered with tiles that are no longer smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceiling tiles are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

Turkey, which was prepared in advance (day prior), was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Turkey observed with temperatures between 53- and 55-degrees F in the walk-in cooler. Turkey was voluntarily disposed of during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Large pieces of turkey were placed hot in the refrigerator in covered pans. FMC is to review proper cooling methods with cooking staff. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
Refrigerator door gasket of the 1-door reach-in observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gasket is to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine, soda/juice dispenser units, and shelving have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024
The ceiling in areas of the kitchen is covered with tiles that are no longer smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceiling tiles are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

Some sections of the walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Service calls have been placed several times since last inspection. Service has yet to occur. Service call placed during inspection with inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2023

Ambient temperature of 3-door reach-in #1 observed at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Turkey observed with a temperature of 44 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Small amount of TCS foods in the unit moved to walk-in cooler. Service has been done on this unit since the last inspection. New service call placed during this inspection. No TCS foods are to be placed in this unit until it is a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta (made today - 2 hours prior to inspection) was placed hot in the refrigerator in a covered container greater than four inches deep. Temperature observed at 68 degrees F. Pasta was placed in a shallow pan and placed in refrigerated unit to complete the cooling process. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black/brown residues. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Service call placed and ice machine is being drained to be properly cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 - 300 ppm. Service call has been placed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Ambient temperature of 3-door reach-in #1 was observed held at 42 - 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Foods in this unit observed with temperatures between 42 - 43 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Service call has been placed. TCS foods are not to be stored in this unit until it is repaired and maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a dead mouse observed under the dry storage rack. No droppings observed in/around food/food equipment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per interview, kitchen areas are checked daily, and dead rodents are removed. Facility does have active pest control. Pest control notified and service is to be increased until this issue is resolved. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023
Refrigerator door gasket of 3-door reach-in #1 observed in poor repair and must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2023
3-bay sink observed to be leaking. Plumbing must be maintained in good repair. Leak must be properly repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2023
A couple of ceiling tiles are missing in the kitchen and dry storage room. Ceiling tiles must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2023
